name: Reflective Git Geometry Contract
core_law: A commit is not only a change; it is a routed event in repository geometry.
secondary_law: A repository learns when repeated routed events become constraints, validators, evidence surfaces, and future routing rules.

node_definition.tag: Tag pointing to this commit, if any.
node_definition.meridians: source, validation, evidence, drift, agent, safety, runtime, memory, release, documentation.
node_definition.sectors: core, schemas, runtime, validation, evidence, rcc, agent, examples, release, memory, documentation.
node_definition.lesson_ids_detected: CMS-L-* lesson IDs detected in commit message or changed files.
node_definition.validator_reports_touched: Validation report surfaces changed by commit.
node_definition.release_truth: Whether current validator state supports repository-bound release truth.

rules: Every geometry node must include commit, route, changed files, and release-truth context.
Geometry classification is repository orientation, not proof of correctness.
Feedback lifecycle v0.3b must attach feedback objects to Git geometry coordinates.
non_claim_lock: Reflective Git geometry improves repository orientation, routing, and evidence traceability. It does not prove code correctness, truth, AGI, consciousness, production readiness, security, external validation, or real-world correctness.
